summary: An OSINT tool designed for crawling and visualizing data from the Tor network, providing link relationship mapping and site information gathering.
risk_notes: Requires Python 3.9+ and dependencies via pip or Docker. Running crawlers against dark web sites carries inherent security and privacy risks; users should ensure they are operating within a secure, isolated environment (e.g., a dedicated VM or container) and understand the implications of interacting with Tor hidden services.

title: qasimwani/spatial-reasoning
summary: A Python package and CLI tool that simplifies object detection by leveraging various reasoning and vision models like OpenAI and Gemini.

title: voltagent/awesome-codex-subagents

The agents themselves are configuration files that define behavior; they do not execute code directly but instruct the underlying Codex model. Security risk depends on the 'sandbox_mode' defined in the TOML (read-only vs workspace-write), which dictates whether the AI can modify your local files.

title: roboflow/sports

risk_notes: Installs via pip from git source. Requires Python 3.8+. It is a library, so it runs within your local environment's permissions. It does not require root, but will likely require significant compute resources (GPU) and dependencies like OpenCV or PyTorch, which are common vectors for supply chain issues if not pinned correctly.

title: budgetbee/budgetbee

risk_notes: Installed via Docker Compose, which is a standard and secure practice. The application requires a MySQL database and handles sensitive financial data; it should be deployed behind a reverse proxy with SSL. It is under active development, so users should be cautious of potential breaking changes or bugs.

title: pku-yuangroup/ultrashape-1.0

Installation involves compiling custom CUDA extensions (cubvh, pytorch3d, torch_cluster) which requires a GPU and appropriate drivers. The code executes arbitrary model weights and scripts locally; it does not require root access but will access the local filesystem for input/output processing.

The tool performs concurrent HTTP requests to external websites; it does not require root privileges or API keys, but it does generate network traffic to a large number of third-party domains. The code is open-source and relies on standard Python libraries (requests, termcolor).

title: bansheetech/homedockos
summary: HomeDock OS is a web-based desktop environment and management layer for self-hosted applications, designed to provide a unified, window-managed interface for Docker-based services across various platforms.
risk_notes: The project relies on Docker/Docker Compose for deployment, which requires root or sudo privileges on the host system. It exposes a web interface that manages other containers, meaning it has broad access to the host's Docker socket and potentially the underlying filesystem. Users should review the provided docker-compose.yml files before deployment to understand volume mappings and network exposure.

title: exadel-inc/compreface
summary: A robust, open-source face recognition system that provides a REST API for detection, verification, and analysis, easily deployable via Docker.
risk_notes: Deployed via docker-compose, which is a standard and safe installation method. It requires network access to serve the API and local storage for face data. As a biometric processing tool, it handles sensitive data, so it should be isolated within a secure network segment. The project is actively maintained by Exadel.

title: vast-ai-research/anigen
summary: AniGen is a research-grade framework for generating fully rigged, animate-ready 3D assets from single images using unified S3 fields.
risk_notes: Requires significant GPU resources (18GB+ VRAM) and CUDA dependencies. Installation involves running a shell script (setup.sh) that manages environment creation and package installation. As with most research code, it may have unvetted dependencies and requires careful isolation in a virtual environment.

title: ysharma3501/luxtts
summary: A highly efficient, lightweight text-to-speech model capable of high-quality voice cloning at speeds exceeding 150x realtime, suitable for local GPU or CPU inference.
risk_notes: Standard Python installation via pip. Requires downloading model weights from Hugging Face, which involves network access. Runs locally on the user's machine, so it has access to the local filesystem and hardware (GPU/CPU). No root access or external API keys required for core functionality.

title: firecrawl/open-scouts
summary: An AI-powered web monitoring platform that uses Firecrawl and OpenAI to automate web searches and send email notifications via scheduled tasks.
risk_notes: Requires multiple third-party API keys (OpenAI, Firecrawl, Resend) and a Supabase backend. Installation involves running database setup scripts via the Supabase CLI and deploying edge functions to the cloud. The application handles sensitive credentials via Supabase Vault and requires network access to external APIs.

title: tbxark/mail2telegram
summary: A serverless Cloudflare Worker that forwards incoming emails to a Telegram bot, providing a convenient way to handle temporary email addresses and notifications.
risk_notes: Deployed as a Cloudflare Worker; requires a Telegram Bot Token and Cloudflare Email Routing configuration. It handles sensitive email data, so ensure the Telegram Bot ID is restricted to your own account. Maintenance is active, and the code is standard TypeScript for the Cloudflare Workers runtime.

title: silentchainai/silentchain
summary: A powerful Burp Suite extension that integrates various AI models for passive vulnerability scanning, featuring built-in data sanitization to protect sensitive information.
risk_notes: Installed as a Java extension (.jar) within Burp Suite. It requires access to your HTTP traffic and potentially external API keys (OpenAI, Claude, etc.) if not using local Ollama. It includes a 'DataSanitizer' feature to redact sensitive info, but users should verify its efficacy before processing highly sensitive production traffic. The project is actively maintained.

title: tommyjepsen/awesome-ux-skills
summary: A collection of prompt-based 'skills' for Claude Code that automate UX research, UI analysis, and AI product design workflows.
risk_notes: The installation script copies markdown files into the ~/.claude/skills/ directory. It requires local filesystem access to the Claude configuration folder. The script is a simple shell script, but users should inspect install.sh before execution. No network access or API keys are required beyond what Claude Code already uses.

title: yibie/skills-manager
summary: A native macOS application and TUI that centralizes the management, discovery, and installation of skills for various AI coding agents.
risk_notes: Distributed as a macOS app bundle or via npm for the TUI. The app requires read/write access to agent configuration files and skill directories on the local filesystem. It performs network requests to fetch skill data from skills.sh and supports local LLM sandbox testing. It is a local-first application with no external backend, but users should be aware that it modifies agent-specific configuration files.

title: celadaniel/free-ai-resources-x
summary: A well-structured, curated collection of high-quality, free AI learning resources, tools, and datasets organized by skill level and domain.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository (a list of links). There is no code to execute, no installation required, and no security risk to your local machine or infrastructure.

title: rotki/rotki
summary: A privacy-focused, self-hosted portfolio management and accounting tool for cryptocurrency and blockchain assets that keeps all financial data local.
risk_notes: The application is available as pre-packaged binaries or via Docker, which is the recommended approach for isolation. It requires read-only API keys or public addresses to track exchange and blockchain data. As a financial tool, it handles sensitive data locally, so users should ensure their local storage is encrypted and backups are secure. It is actively maintained with frequent updates.

title: nexu-io/html-video
summary: A programmatic video generation tool that acts as a meta-layer for coding agents, allowing users to convert HTML/CSS and data into MP4s using various rendering engines.
risk_notes: Requires local installation of Node.js and FFmpeg. As it is designed to be used with AI coding agents, it will likely require access to your local filesystem and potentially API keys for LLM providers (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI). It runs locally on your machine, which is a positive security posture compared to cloud-based video services.

title: ar51an/unbound-dashboard
summary: A comprehensive monitoring stack for Unbound DNS, providing a custom Go-based exporter, Grafana dashboards, and Prometheus/Loki configuration for detailed DNS analytics.
risk_notes: Requires root access to install systemd services and modify Unbound configuration files. Installation involves manual binary placement and configuration of system services. The exporter runs as a background process and requires read access to Unbound's unix socket. No automated 'curl-to-bash' scripts are used, which is a positive security signal.

title: instavm/open-skills
summary: An MCP-compliant server that enables local execution of Anthropic's 'Claude Skills' within sandboxed Apple native containers, ensuring data privacy for file processing tasks.
risk_notes: Installation involves running a shell script (chmod +x install.sh). The tool executes AI-generated code locally in sandboxed containers, which limits risk, but it requires access to your local filesystem to process documents. It acts as an MCP server, meaning it will interface with your AI clients (Claude Desktop, etc.) and potentially execute arbitrary code on your machine based on LLM instructions.

title: joyceql/magic-resume
summary: A modern, AI-assisted resume editor built with TanStack Start that supports real-time preview, custom themes, and self-hosting via Docker.
risk_notes: Standard Node.js/Docker installation. Requires API keys for AI features. The license includes specific commercial restrictions despite being Apache 2.0, which requires attention if used in a business context. Active development and community support are present.

title: kittendevv/invio
summary: A minimalist, self-hosted invoicing application designed to simplify billing by providing secure, account-free invoice links for clients.
risk_notes: Typically deployed via Docker/containerization, which is standard for self-hosting. Requires database connectivity and persistent storage for invoice data. As a web-facing application, it requires proper reverse proxy configuration and security hardening to prevent unauthorized access to financial records.

title: coderluii/holyclaude
summary: A comprehensive, pre-configured Docker-based AI development workstation that bundles Claude Code, multiple AI CLIs, and a headless browser for a seamless self-hosted coding environment.
risk_notes: Installed via Docker Compose. Requires bind-mounting local directories for persistence and access to your Anthropic API keys or OAuth credentials. As an AI coding agent, it has full read/write access to the directories you mount to the container, which could allow it to modify or delete files on your host system if the AI is compromised or misconfigured.

title: naituw/ipapatch
summary: A template Xcode project that enables injecting custom code, frameworks, and debugging capabilities into decrypted iOS IPA files without requiring a jailbroken device.
risk_notes: Requires Xcode and Apple developer signing certificates to build and deploy to physical iOS devices. The tool involves modifying binary IPA files, which requires decrypted binaries; users must be aware of the legal and security implications of reverse-engineering third-party applications. It is a local build tool, not a service, so it does not inherently require network access or secrets, but the injected code can perform any action the host app is permitted to do.

title: x011/smtp-tunnel-proxy
summary: A covert SOCKS5 proxy tool that encapsulates traffic within SMTP-like streams to bypass restrictive firewalls and deep packet inspection.
risk_notes: The installation uses a 'curl | sudo bash' script, which grants full root access to the server. The tool requires opening port 587 on the host and managing TLS certificates. As a proxy, it handles all traffic routed through it, meaning the server operator can theoretically inspect or log unencrypted traffic passing through the tunnel.

title: amaancoderx/npxskillui
summary: A powerful CLI tool that reverse-engineers websites or codebases into structured design systems, specifically formatted for Claude Code to automate UI generation.
risk_notes: Installed via npm (npm install -g skillui). Requires Node.js 18+. The 'ultra' mode uses Playwright, which executes a browser engine on your machine. It performs network requests to crawl URLs and reads local files when scanning directories. It does not require API keys, but users should be cautious when running it against untrusted local codebases or repositories.

title: shridarpatil/whatomate
summary: A self-hosted, open-source WhatsApp Business platform that provides multi-tenant messaging, chatbot automation, and analytics via a single Go binary or Docker container.
risk_notes: Installs via Docker Compose or a standalone binary. Requires integration with Meta's WhatsApp Cloud API (requires API keys) and a PostgreSQL database. The application handles sensitive customer communication data and requires network exposure to function as a webhook receiver for WhatsApp.

title: llmsresearch/paperbanana
summary: An agentic framework for automating the creation of academic diagrams and statistical plots from text or PDF inputs, featuring a CLI, Python API, and MCP server support.
risk_notes: Installed via pip or Docker. Requires external API keys (OpenAI, Azure, or Google Gemini) to function. As an agentic tool, it processes local files and interacts with LLM providers, so it should be run with restricted filesystem access if processing sensitive research data.

title: foru17/neko-master
summary: A modern, Docker-based dashboard for visualizing and auditing network traffic from OpenClash or similar gateway backends.
risk_notes: Distributed via Docker image. Requires mounting local volumes for data persistence and GeoIP databases. Accesses network traffic metadata from connected proxy backends. Security posture is standard for a web-based dashboard; requires a secure COOKIE_SECRET and should be placed behind a reverse proxy with authentication if exposed externally.

title: brobertsaz/claude-os
summary: Claude OS is a persistent memory layer for Claude Code, allowing the AI to retain project-specific knowledge, architecture patterns, and session state across multiple interactions using a SQLite-backed knowledge base.
risk_notes: Requires local Python environment (3.11/3.12) and SQLite. It interacts with your local filesystem to index code and store memories. It requires API keys for Claude/OpenAI and potentially Ollama for local models. It is actively maintained with frequent updates and community contributions, though it should be treated as a tool with read/write access to your project directories.

title: tradermonty/claude-trading-skills
summary: A structured toolkit for integrating Claude with trading workflows, focusing on decision-making, risk management, and journaling rather than automated execution.
risk_notes: Requires manual integration with Claude's 'Skills' feature. Optional API keys (Alpaca, FMP) are required for automated data fetching, which introduces potential credential exposure risks. The project is modular, allowing for manual, non-API usage, which significantly reduces the attack surface.

title: voltagent/awesome-claude-skills
summary: A comprehensive, curated directory of agent skills and tool definitions for popular AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI.
risk_notes: This is a documentation/curation repository, not a binary or executable. It contains links and configuration schemas for AI agents. Users should exercise caution when importing third-party tool definitions into AI agents, as these tools may be granted access to local files, environment variables, or cloud API keys by the agent runtime.

title: open-finance-lab/agentictrading
summary: An experimental platform for building, backtesting, and paper-trading with LLM-powered financial agents, featuring a FastAPI backend and a web-based dashboard.
risk_notes: Requires local configuration of API keys (e.g., Alpaca, LLM providers) stored in a local credentials file. The application runs a local FastAPI server and interacts with external financial APIs. It is a research-oriented project, so users should audit the LLM integration code before connecting to live paper-trading accounts.

title: conway-research/automaton
summary: An experimental framework for autonomous AI agents that manage their own compute costs, self-replicate, and modify their own code.
risk_notes: Extremely high risk. The project requires full shell execution, filesystem access, and on-chain wallet management. It explicitly supports self-modification of its own source code and uses a 'curl | sh' installation method. It is designed to operate autonomously with financial capabilities, making it a significant security liability for any home-lab or production environment.

title: freeu-group/freetodo
summary: FreeTodo is an AI-powered task management application that combines a FastAPI backend with a Next.js frontend to provide intelligent task decomposition and context-aware scheduling.
risk_notes: Installation involves running scripts from the internet (curl/iwr) and managing Python/Node environments. The application requires local filesystem access for SQLite/ChromaDB storage and likely requires external API keys (e.g., OpenAI/LLM providers) to function as an 'AI assistant'. It is a self-hosted service that exposes a local network port.

title: ankane/awesome-legal
summary: A curated collection of open-source and free legal document templates for startups, covering everything from NDAs to investor agreements.
risk_notes: This is a static documentation repository with no code to execute, install, or run. It poses no security risk to your local machine or infrastructure. It is a reference list of external links.

title: symgraph/idassist
summary: An advanced AI-powered plugin for IDA Pro that integrates LLMs, RAG, and knowledge graphs to automate binary reverse engineering tasks.
risk_notes: Requires installation into IDA Pro's Python environment via pip. It necessitates API keys for LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and has the capability to modify the IDB database and execute external tools via MCP. It is actively maintained and follows standard plugin installation patterns for IDA.

title: virattt/ai-financial-agent
summary: A TypeScript-based AI financial research agent template that uses Vercel's AI SDK and the Financial Datasets API to provide interactive stock analysis and data visualization.
risk_notes: Requires multiple external API keys (OpenAI, Financial Datasets, LangSmith) managed via a .env file. Installation is standard via pnpm/npm. The project is a template/proof-of-concept and requires careful handling of API credentials to avoid unauthorized usage or billing spikes.

title: angristan/netclode
summary: A sophisticated self-hosted coding agent platform that uses Kata Containers and microVMs for secure, isolated AI-driven development, accessible via a native iOS/macOS app.
risk_notes: Requires significant infrastructure setup including a VPS with nested virtualization support, k3s, and Tailscale. Installation is managed via Ansible playbooks. The system handles sensitive GitHub tokens and LLM API keys, though it includes a proxy to prevent these from entering the sandbox directly. High complexity and high privilege requirements (root on host, kernel-level isolation).

title: emexlab/emexde
summary: An on-device IDE for iOS that enables native Swift, C, and C++ development directly on unjailbroken iPhones using a kernel virtualization layer.
risk_notes: Requires sideloading an IPA file, which involves trusting a third-party developer certificate or using a signing service. The app utilizes a kernel virtualization layer to execute code, which is a high-privilege operation that bypasses standard iOS sandboxing constraints. Use with caution as it requires full access to the device's filesystem and execution environment.

title: 0-ai-ug/cate
summary: An infinite-canvas desktop IDE that integrates editors, terminals, browsers, and AI agents into a spatial, workspace-oriented interface.
risk_notes: Distributed as prebuilt binaries (DMG, NSIS, AppImage). Requires local filesystem access to manage workspaces and API keys for AI agent functionality. Uses Electron with context isolation and scoped filesystem access, which is a standard security posture for modern IDEs.

title: electroniccats/catsniffer
summary: A versatile, open-source hardware platform for sniffing and analyzing various IoT radio protocols like Zigbee, BLE, and LoRa.
risk_notes: This is a hardware-based tool. Risks involve physical hardware interaction and potential exposure to radio-frequency signals. Software tools (pycatsniffer) are installed via standard Python/pip workflows. It requires USB access to the device, which may necessitate udev rules on Linux. No inherent cloud-based secrets or root-level installation scripts are required.

title: agi-now/buffett-skills
summary: A specialized skill set for Claude Code that implements Warren Buffett's investment framework to provide structured, deep-dive financial analysis.
risk_notes: Installation involves manually cloning the repository and copying files into a local project directory. It requires no external binaries or root access, but it relies on the Claude Code environment. The primary risk is the potential for high token usage due to the systematic reading of multiple reference files during analysis.

title: usekaneo/kaneo
summary: Kaneo is a lightweight, self-hosted project management tool designed as a minimalist alternative to Jira or Linear, built with TypeScript and Hono.
risk_notes: Offers a 'drim' CLI tool that uses a 'curl | sh' installation method, which requires caution. Standard Docker Compose deployment is available and preferred for better isolation. Requires PostgreSQL and environment variable management for secrets (AUTH_SECRET).

title: repowise-dev/repowise
summary: A powerful codebase intelligence tool that provides dependency graphs, git analytics, and automated refactoring plans via MCP, specifically designed to optimize AI coding agents.
risk_notes: Installed via pip (Python 3.11+). It requires read access to your local codebase to build its index and graph. As an MCP-compatible tool, it integrates with LLM agents, meaning it may expose your codebase structure and metadata to the AI models you use. The project is actively maintained and uses AGPL-3.0 licensing.

title: koide3/hdl_graph_slam
summary: A robust, widely-used ROS package for real-time 3D LIDAR-based Graph SLAM, supporting various sensor constraints like GPS, IMU, and floor plane detection.
risk_notes: Requires ROS (Robot Operating System) environment. Installation involves cloning source code into a catkin workspace and building with catkin_make. It relies on several system-level dependencies (PCL, g2o, suitesparse) installed via apt. It is a specialized research-grade tool with no inherent network access, but it processes raw sensor data streams.

title: kebo-ai/kebo
summary: A React Native/Expo-based personal finance and budgeting mobile application that integrates with Supabase for backend services.
risk_notes: Requires setting up a personal Supabase instance and managing API keys for PostHog and Supabase. The project is a mobile app template rather than a self-hosted server-side tool, which does not align well with the user's interest in home-lab/automation/data pipelines.

title: infinition/bjorn
summary: Bjorn is an autonomous, Tamagotchi-inspired offensive security tool designed for Raspberry Pi, featuring network scanning, vulnerability assessment, and automated exploitation capabilities.
risk_notes: The installation uses a 'curl | sudo bash' pattern, which is high-risk as it executes arbitrary code with root privileges. The tool is designed for offensive security, meaning it performs network scanning, brute-forcing, and data exfiltration; it should only be used in controlled, authorized environments. It requires significant system access to function as a network auditor.

title: linnykoleh/awesome-system-design
summary: A curated collection of links to system design courses, articles, books, and videos for interview preparation and architectural learning.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository containing links to external resources. There is no code to execute, no installation required, and no security risk associated with the repository itself.

title: viarotel-org/escrcpy
summary: A feature-rich Electron-based GUI for scrcpy that adds advanced device management, keyboard mapping, and AI-assisted automation for Android devices.
risk_notes: Installs via pre-built binaries or Homebrew. Requires ADB (Android Debug Bridge) access to your device, which grants the application significant control over the phone (screen capture, input simulation, file transfer, and shell command execution). The project includes a 'paid' extension model for advanced features, which may introduce closed-source components.

title: taskosaur/taskosaur
summary: A self-hosted project management platform that integrates conversational AI for task execution and workflow automation.
risk_notes: Uses Docker Compose for deployment, which is standard and secure. Requires external API keys for LLM integration (OpenAI/Anthropic/etc.) and handles sensitive project data. The project uses a Business Source License (BSL), which is more restrictive than standard open-source licenses. Requires PostgreSQL and Redis as dependencies.

title: fougue/mayo
summary: Mayo is a cross-platform, open-source 3D CAD viewer and converter built on Qt and OpenCascade, featuring a robust CLI for batch file format conversion.
risk_notes: Distributed as pre-built binaries for Windows/Linux/macOS or via package managers like Winget/Scoop. It is a desktop application that runs locally with standard user permissions; it requires filesystem access to read/write CAD files. No network access is required for core functionality. The project is actively maintained.

title: vitalets/awesome-smart-tv
summary: A comprehensive curated list of resources, SDKs, and third-party libraries for developing applications and remote control interfaces for major Smart TV platforms.
risk_notes: This is a curated list of links, not software itself. Individual projects linked within the list vary significantly in risk; some are Node.js modules or Python scripts that require network access to your local TV, while others are official SDKs. Always audit third-party remote control libraries for network permissions and potential credential handling before execution.

title: taoidle/plan-cascade
summary: A multi-modal AI development framework that decomposes complex software projects into parallel, executable tasks using a cascading architecture.
risk_notes: Requires API keys for LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, etc.) or local Ollama access. Installation involves pip or plugin managers, which can execute arbitrary code in your environment. As an AI agent framework, it has full read/write access to your local filesystem and can execute shell commands; use with caution in sensitive project directories.

title: finrandojin/alexandria-audiobook
summary: A comprehensive AI-powered audiobook generation pipeline that handles script annotation, multi-voice synthesis, voice cloning, and LoRA training, with export options for M4B and Audacity.
risk_notes: Requires significant local resources (GPU with 8GB+ VRAM recommended). Installation is typically managed via Pinokio or standard Python environment setup. It requires access to local LLM servers (Ollama/LM Studio) or external API keys (OpenAI). As it processes local files and generates audio, it requires read/write access to your filesystem. The project is actively maintained but involves running complex machine learning models locally.

title: adolfousier/agentverse
summary: A Rust-based multi-agent orchestration platform that provides a visual (3D or TUI) world and REST API for managing and coordinating AI agents.
risk_notes: Installed via 'cargo install', which pulls from crates.io. Runs a local HTTP server on port 18800 and requires API key authentication. It persists data to '~/.config/agentverse/agentverse.db' using SQLite. The project is very new (March 2026) and should be treated as experimental.

title: tronghieuit/tiny-tts
summary: An ultra-lightweight, high-performance English text-to-speech engine with only 1.6M parameters, optimized for CPU inference on edge devices and home-lab hardware.
risk_notes: Installs via standard pip or npm. The model downloads external assets (ONNX weights) from HuggingFace on first use. It runs locally on the CPU, requiring standard filesystem access for reading checkpoints and writing audio files. No elevated privileges or API keys are required.

title: aidanpark/openclaw-android
summary: A lightweight method to run OpenClaw on Android devices by using a glibc dynamic linker instead of a full proot-distro Linux environment, significantly reducing storage and performance overhead.
risk_notes: Installation involves a 'curl | bash' script which executes arbitrary code in the Termux environment. It requires network access to download binaries and filesystem access to manage the OpenClaw runtime. The project is actively maintained, but running external binaries via a custom glibc loader on Android carries inherent security risks regarding binary integrity and potential privilege escalation within the Termux sandbox.

title: addyosmani/agentic-seo
summary: A CLI tool for auditing documentation and websites for 'Agentic Engine Optimization' (AEO), ensuring content is structured correctly for AI coding agents to consume.
risk_notes: Installed via npm/npx. It performs local filesystem analysis and can crawl live URLs. It does not require API keys as it uses local heuristic analysis. The project is very new but maintained by a well-known developer in the web ecosystem.

title: claration/impactor
summary: A cross-platform, Rust-based iOS sideloading tool that simplifies signing and installing IPA files, managing certificates, and injecting tweaks.
risk_notes: Requires providing Apple ID credentials to sign apps, which is a significant trust requirement. Installation is via standard package managers (Homebrew, Flatpak) or direct binaries. It interacts with system-level USB drivers (usbmuxd on Linux, iTunes drivers on Windows) and requires local storage of signing keys/certificates.

title: dev-ali2008/xanite
summary: An Android-based proof-of-concept emulator for the original Xbox, written in C.
risk_notes: Distributed as an Android application (APK). Requires broad filesystem access to read game ISOs and potentially sensitive system permissions. The project is a proof-of-concept with high open issue counts and limited documentation, posing potential stability and security risks typical of early-stage emulation software.

title: llnl/oghidra
summary: OGhidra is an agentic framework that integrates LLMs with the Ghidra reverse engineering platform to automate binary analysis, function renaming, and pattern detection.
risk_notes: Requires local installation of Ghidra, Java, and Ollama. The tool runs as a Python application and interacts with Ghidra via a custom plugin; it requires API keys if using cloud LLMs. It performs automated analysis on binary files, which inherently involves executing potentially malicious code in a sandbox environment. Maintained by LLNL, suggesting a professional development standard.

title: hwdsl2/wireguard-install
summary: A highly popular and well-maintained shell script that automates the installation and configuration of a WireGuard VPN server on various Linux distributions.
risk_notes: Requires root privileges (sudo) to install and configure network interfaces, firewall rules, and system services. The installation method involves downloading and executing a shell script via curl/wget, which is a common but high-privilege pattern; users should audit the script before execution. It modifies system-level network settings and requires opening UDP ports on the host firewall.

title: rshah515/claude-code-subagents
summary: A comprehensive library of 165+ specialized system prompt files designed to be used as subagents within Anthropic's Claude Code CLI tool.
risk_notes: This is a collection of text-based prompt files (Markdown). There is no executable code, so the risk is minimal. Usage requires the Claude Code CLI, which requires an Anthropic API key and grants the AI agent access to your local filesystem and shell execution capabilities. Users should review the prompt files before use to ensure they align with their security preferences.

title: hrishikesh7665/android-pentesting-checklist
summary: A comprehensive, well-organized checklist and resource guide for conducting security assessments and penetration testing on Android applications.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository (Markdown files), so it carries no inherent execution risk. However, the tools it recommends (Frida, Magisk, Drozer, MobSF) require significant system-level access, including root privileges on test devices and the ability to intercept network traffic. Users should exercise caution when running these tools on production devices.

title: frectonz/sql-studio
summary: A high-performance, single-binary SQL database explorer written in Rust that supports a wide range of databases including SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and DuckDB.
risk_notes: Installation via curl/powershell script is common but requires caution; binary can be audited or installed via Nix. It requires network access to connect to remote databases and filesystem access to read local database files. It handles sensitive database credentials, so it should be run in a secure environment.

title: johnjago/awesome-free-software
summary: A comprehensive, long-standing curated list of free and open-source software across various categories, useful for discovering tools for home-lab and development environments.
risk_notes: This is a documentation repository containing links to external software. It does not contain executable code itself. Users should exercise standard caution when installing software found in the list, as each project has its own unique installation method (e.g., package managers, binaries, or scripts) and varying security/permission requirements.

title: frondent/polymarketbtc15massistant
summary: A console-based trading assistant for Polymarket BTC 15-minute markets that provides technical analysis and price feeds.
risk_notes: Requires Node.js/npm installation. The tool connects to external WebSocket and HTTP endpoints (Polymarket, Binance, Polygon RPCs). It is a very new, niche repository with no license and minimal history, posing a high risk for financial tool usage. It does not require root, but users should be cautious about the code's interaction with financial data.

title: dosaygo-studio/hackerbook
summary: A fully static, client-side searchable archive of Hacker News using SQLite WASM, designed for offline access and long-term data preservation.
risk_notes: Requires Node.js 20+ and Google Cloud SDK for the full ETL pipeline. The viewer mode is a standard static site served via local HTTP server (e.g., python3 -m http.server), which is low-risk. The archivist mode requires GCP credentials to pull data from BigQuery. The project is actively maintained and uses standard npm workflows.

title: ages2001/universal-nt-installer
summary: A specialized Linux-based utility for rapidly deploying legacy Windows NT operating systems (NT 3.1 through XP) onto modern or legacy hardware.
risk_notes: The tool is a bootable environment (Tiny Core Linux based) that performs low-level disk partitioning and formatting. It requires physical access to the target machine and carries high risk of data loss on the target drive. It is intended for offline use and does not require network access, but it is a niche project in beta stage with limited maintenance.

title: fufankeji/deepseek-ocr-web
summary: A web-based studio for document parsing and OCR using the DeepSeek-OCR model, featuring a React frontend and FastAPI backend.
risk_notes: Requires significant hardware resources (GPU with >=7GB VRAM). Installation involves running shell scripts (install.sh/start.sh) which should be audited before execution. The application requires local model weights and exposes a web interface, necessitating careful network configuration if exposed publicly. No license is specified, which may pose legal risks for commercial use.

title: gu1llaum-3/sshm
summary: A modern, TUI-based SSH configuration manager written in Go that provides an interactive interface for managing hosts, port forwarding, and connection history while respecting existing ~/.ssh/config files.
risk_notes: Installs via curl-to-bash or binary download. It requires read/write access to your ~/.ssh/config file and filesystem to manage SSH keys and configurations. title: nour833/stegoforge

summary: Scanopy is a self-hosted network discovery and documentation tool that automatically generates L2, L3, workload, and application dependency diagrams by scanning your infrastructure.
risk_notes: Installed via Docker Compose or Proxmox helper scripts. As a network scanner, it requires broad network access to perform SNMP, ARP, and service discovery across your infrastructure. It should be treated as a high-privilege service within your network. The project is actively maintained and uses Rust for the core scanner, which is a positive security signal.

title: rltvty2/ulli
summary: ULLI is a utility that automates the installation of various Linux distributions directly from a running Windows or Linux environment, bypassing the need for physical USB installation media.
risk_notes: High-risk utility. Requires root/administrator privileges to modify partition tables, UEFI boot entries, and disk structures. Improper use can render the system unbootable. Installation involves downloading scripts and running them with sudo or as administrator. Requires disabling security features like BitLocker and Secure Boot for full functionality.

title: chrisbenincasa/tunarr
summary: Tunarr is a powerful self-hosted tool that allows users to create custom live TV channels from their existing media libraries in Plex, Jellyfin, or Emby, complete with scheduling and hardware-accelerated transcoding.
risk_notes: Distributed primarily via Docker, which is a standard and secure deployment method. It requires read access to your media libraries and network access to your media server (Plex/Jellyfin/Emby). It does not require root privileges if run via Docker. The project is actively maintained with a healthy community and clear documentation.

title: weirdmachine64/ghidragpt
summary: A Ghidra plugin that integrates various LLM providers to assist in reverse engineering tasks like code explanation, vulnerability detection, and refactoring.
risk_notes: Requires building from source using Maven and installing as a Ghidra extension. It requires external API keys for LLM providers, which are stored locally; users should be cautious when sending proprietary or sensitive binary code to third-party AI services. The project is actively maintained.

title: longbridge/longbridge-terminal
summary: A robust, AI-native CLI and TUI for the Longbridge trading platform, offering real-time market data, portfolio management, and order execution with JSON output support for automation.
risk_notes: Installs via curl-to-shell or Homebrew. Requires OAuth 2.0 authentication to access a financial trading account; the tool stores credentials locally. As it performs financial transactions, users should exercise caution regarding the permissions granted to the API tokens.

title: qosf/awesome-quantum-software
summary: A comprehensive curated list of open-source quantum computing software, libraries, and simulators.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository containing links to external projects. It poses no direct security risk as it contains no executable code, but users should exercise caution when installing the various third-party quantum SDKs linked within it, as they may require system-level dependencies or API keys for cloud-based quantum hardware.

title: ni5arga/sightline
summary: A geospatial OSINT search engine that leverages OpenStreetMap and the Overpass API to map and analyze critical infrastructure like power plants, data centers, and telecommunications sites.
risk_notes: The project is a web application (TypeScript/Next.js) that interacts with public OSM APIs. It does not require local root access or sensitive credentials to run, but it performs external network requests to Nominatim and Overpass API. It is actively maintained and follows standard web development patterns.

title: mafyuh/iac
summary: A comprehensive, GitOps-driven infrastructure-as-code repository for managing a complex homelab environment using Talos, Kubernetes, Proxmox, and Ansible.
risk_notes: This is a configuration repository, not a standalone application. It requires significant manual setup and understanding of the underlying tools (Kubernetes, Flux, Ansible). It does not contain executable binaries but implies the use of sensitive credentials (secrets) which should be managed via tools like SOPS or SealedSecrets. Maintenance is active, but users must audit the YAML files before applying them to their own infrastructure.

title: ashishpatel26/500-ai-machine-learning-deep-learning-computer-vision-nlp-projects-with-code
summary: A large, curated list of links to various AI, machine learning, and data science project tutorials and blog posts.
risk_notes: This is a collection of links to external websites and third-party repositories. There is no central installation method, but users should exercise caution when clicking through to external blogs or running code snippets from unverified third-party sources, as they may contain outdated dependencies or security vulnerabilities.

title: aiming-lab/simplemem
summary: SimpleMem is a Python-based library and MCP server that provides efficient, long-term memory capabilities for LLM agents, supporting both text and multimodal data through semantic compression.
risk_notes: Installed via pip. Requires API keys for LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) or local inference backends. As an agentic tool, it has the potential to read/write to files and interact with external APIs depending on the agent configuration; users should review the memory storage path and access permissions.

title: saccofrancesco/deepshot
summary: An AI-powered NBA game outcome predictor that uses EWMA statistics and XGBoost, featuring a NiceGUI-based web interface for local visualization.
risk_notes: Standard Python project installation via pip requirements.txt. It requires local network access to scrape Basketball Reference and runs a local web server via NiceGUI. No sensitive credentials are required, but it executes arbitrary Python code via Jupyter Notebooks for model training.

title: magnum6actual/flipoff
summary: A lightweight, zero-dependency web application that emulates a mechanical split-flap display for use on TVs or monitors.
risk_notes: Extremely low risk. It is a static HTML/CSS/JS project with no external dependencies or build tools. It runs entirely client-side in the browser and requires no network access or sensitive permissions.

title: tranvuongquocdat/sidescreen
summary: An open-source utility that allows users to extend their macOS desktop to an Android tablet via USB-C or Wi-Fi, functioning similarly to Apple's Sidecar but for non-Apple hardware.
risk_notes: Requires downloading and installing a .dmg on macOS and an .apk on Android. The app requires USB debugging permissions on Android and potentially accessibility/screen recording permissions on macOS to function as a display. It uses ADB for USB connections, which is a standard developer tool. The project is actively maintained.

title: htdt/godogen
summary: An autonomous agent framework for game development that leverages LLMs to scaffold, build, and iterate on projects in Godot, Bevy, and Babylon.js.
risk_notes: Requires multiple third-party API keys (Gemini, xAI, Tripo3D) and execution of shell scripts (publish.sh). The agent has full filesystem access to the generated project directories and requires local installation of game engines and build tools (Rust, Node.js, Godot). It is designed to run long-term processes, often on GPU-enabled servers, which necessitates careful monitoring of API usage costs and system resource consumption.

title: microsoft/data-science-for-beginners
summary: A comprehensive 10-week educational curriculum for learning data science fundamentals using Python and Jupyter Notebooks.
risk_notes: This is a collection of educational content and Jupyter notebooks. There is no software to install or execute, so it carries no security risk. It is not a developer tool or automation utility, which falls outside the user's primary interest in technical tooling.

title: mustbeperfect/definitive-opensource
summary: A highly curated, community-driven directory of high-quality, actively maintained consumer and self-hosted open source software.
risk_notes: This is a documentation repository and does not contain executable code that requires installation. It is a static resource list. No security risks are associated with browsing the repository or its associated website.

title: compassmb/mbcompass
summary: A lightweight, open-source Android compass and navigation app that focuses on privacy and offline usage.
risk_notes: Distributed via F-Droid or APK sideloading. Requires location permissions and foreground service notifications. No network access required for core functionality, though it does not appear to be a developer tool or infrastructure project relevant to the user's home-lab/automation interests.

title: agadiffe/windowsmize
summary: A comprehensive collection of PowerShell scripts designed to automate Windows 10/11 configuration, debloating, telemetry reduction, and application management via Winget.
risk_notes: Requires execution of PowerShell scripts with Administrator privileges. The scripts modify system registry keys, firewall rules, and uninstall system components, which carries a high risk of breaking OS functionality if not reviewed. Users should audit the scripts before execution and create a system restore point first. No external dependencies or API keys required, but it does interact with network settings and system-level services.

title: ciembor/agent-rules-books
summary: A collection of curated, tool-agnostic system prompt rules and skills for AI coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex) derived from classic software engineering literature.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository containing Markdown files. There is no code to execute, no installation script, and no network access required. It is completely safe to use as it simply provides text-based instructions to be pasted into your AI agent's configuration files.

title: openswarm-ai/openswarm
summary: A powerful local orchestrator for managing multiple AI agents in parallel using a spatial dashboard, MCP tool support, and Git worktree isolation.
risk_notes: Installs via pre-built macOS binary or local source (Node/Python). Requires an Anthropic API key. As an agentic tool, it has broad filesystem access (Git worktrees) and executes code/tools on your machine; it is designed to be local-first, but requires careful management of agent permissions and tool access.

title: botswin/botbrowser
summary: A specialized privacy-focused browser core designed for advanced fingerprint protection and automation, supporting Playwright/Puppeteer integration for testing and scraping scenarios.
risk_notes: Distributed as pre-built binaries for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Requires execution of external binaries which have full access to the user's filesystem and network stack. Designed for advanced automation, so it should be run in isolated environments (containers or VMs) to prevent accidental data leakage or system interference. Maintenance is active, but the nature of 'anti-detection' tools often involves cat-and-mouse updates with web services.

title: mariusdjen/vpskit
summary: A comprehensive Bash-based automation tool for provisioning, securing, and managing Linux VPS instances, including Docker deployment, Caddy reverse proxy setup, and automated backups.
risk_notes: Uses a 'curl | bash' installation method which executes remote code with root privileges on your VPS. It requires full SSH access to the target server and performs significant system-level changes (firewall, user creation, SSH hardening). The script is transparent and runs locally, but users should audit the shell script before execution.

title: oopslink/trading-skills
summary: A professional-grade CLI tool for querying Tushare Pro financial data, featuring modular skill architecture, extensive test coverage, and support for multiple output formats.
risk_notes: Requires a Tushare Pro API token, which is stored in a local configuration file or environment variable. Installation is via pip (standard Python package). The tool performs network requests to the Tushare API and writes to the local filesystem for caching purposes. The project is actively maintained with a robust test suite.

title: notharshhaa/devops-projects
summary: A comprehensive collection of hands-on DevOps projects and tutorials covering AWS, CI/CD, Kubernetes, and infrastructure automation.
risk_notes: This is a documentation and tutorial repository, not a software package. It does not require installation, but following the tutorials will require creating and managing cloud infrastructure (AWS) which will incur costs and require handling sensitive API credentials and IAM permissions.

title: ehmo/platform-design-skills
summary: A collection of structured design rules and guidelines for major platforms (Apple, Android, Web) designed to be consumed by AI agents to assist in UI/UX auditing and development.
risk_notes: The project is installed via 'npx', which executes code from the npm registry. It is essentially a documentation and prompt-engineering resource; it does not require elevated privileges or API keys to function, as it is intended to provide context to LLMs.

title: goldskull-777/goldmac
summary: A Python-based CLI tool for automated MAC address rotation and spoofing, designed for Linux-based penetration testing and OPSEC workflows.
risk_notes: Requires root privileges to modify network interface configurations. It relies on system-level dependencies like macchanger and iproute2. The code is executed directly via Python, so users should audit the script before running as root. It is a very new repository with limited community vetting.

title: mental-os/aurora-os.js
summary: A web-based operating system simulator designed as a hacking game, built with React, Vite, and Electron.
risk_notes: This is a browser-based or Electron-packaged game. While it runs in a sandbox, it is a pre-alpha project with limited security auditing. It does not require root access or system-level permissions, but as a complex web application, it should be treated as untrusted code if run locally via Electron.

title: diezo/ensta
summary: A Python library for interacting with Instagram's private mobile and web APIs, allowing for automation of account actions.
risk_notes: This library requires passing raw Instagram credentials (username/password) to the script, which poses a significant security risk. It interacts with undocumented private APIs, making it highly susceptible to breaking changes from Instagram and potential account bans for automated activity. It is not an official tool and relies on reverse-engineered endpoints.

title: roomkangali/droid-llm-hunter
summary: An automated Android security analysis tool that combines static analysis with LLM-based reasoning to identify vulnerabilities and generate proof-of-concept exploits.
risk_notes: Requires Python 3.11+, apktool, and jadx. It performs static analysis on APK files, which involves decompilation and potential execution of untrusted code. It requires API keys for LLM providers (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) to function. The tool is designed for security research and should be run in a sandboxed or isolated environment.

title: samyak2403/repostore
summary: An Android application that acts as a curated app store for GitHub-hosted projects, allowing users to discover, install, and update APKs directly from release assets.
risk_notes: The app is distributed via F-Droid and IzzyOnDroid, which are reputable FOSS repositories. It requires standard Android permissions to install APKs (Package Installer) and network access to query the GitHub API. Optional GitHub OAuth login increases API rate limits but grants the app access to your GitHub account data. As it facilitates installing third-party APKs, users should exercise caution regarding the security of the individual repositories being installed.

title: leoyeai/teammate-skill
summary: An AI agent skill that distills a former teammate's communication style, technical standards, and tribal knowledge into a reusable persona for Claude Code and OpenClaw.
risk_notes: Requires API tokens for Slack and GitHub to perform automated data collection. It processes sensitive historical communication data (Slack, email, PRs), which poses a significant privacy risk if the underlying LLM or agent platform is not configured to handle private data securely. Installation is via git clone or package manager, which is standard, but users should audit the generated skill files to ensure no PII is leaked into public repositories.

title: davesc63/geoport
summary: A cross-platform desktop utility for simulating GPS locations on iOS devices, specifically designed to support newer iOS versions (17/18) on both Windows and macOS.
risk_notes: Requires downloading and running binary executables from GitHub releases. The tool requires administrator/sudo privileges to interface with iOS system services and requires iTunes (on Windows) for USB device communication. It relies on the pymobiledevice3 library to interact with Apple's private protocols.

title: iisu-network/iisu
summary: An Android-based emulation frontend launcher designed for handheld devices, currently in early alpha development.
risk_notes: Distributed as an APK via GitHub releases or Obtainium. As a launcher, it requires broad permissions to access the filesystem (for ROMs/media) and potentially accessibility services or overlay permissions to function as a home screen. It is in early alpha, meaning it may lack security hardening and is prone to bugs.

title: medvedev91/timeto.me
summary: A mobile-focused productivity and time-tracking application that enforces 24/7 goal monitoring.
risk_notes: This is a mobile application distributed via standard app stores (Google Play, Apple App Store, F-Droid). It requires standard mobile permissions for notifications and background activity. It does not fit the user's interest in developer tools, CLI, or home-lab infrastructure.

title: michalparkola/tapestry-skills
summary: A collection of productivity-focused skills for Claude Code, enabling AI agents to extract content from URLs, process YouTube transcripts, and generate structured action plans.
risk_notes: Installs via a shell script that copies files into the user's ~/.claude/skills directory. It relies on external tools like yt-dlp and trafilatura, which require network access to fetch content. The scripts run within the context of the Claude Code agent, meaning they have the same filesystem and execution permissions as the agent itself.

title: nikhilkain/vyxel-apps
summary: An Android application that acts as a curated store for open-source apps hosted on GitHub, featuring automated updates and GitHub star synchronization.
risk_notes: Requires sideloading an APK, which grants the app broad permissions to install other packages on the device. It interacts with the GitHub API and requires network access. As an app installer, it has significant potential impact on device security; users should verify the signature and source.

title: pozzettiandrea/comfyui-unirig
summary: A ComfyUI custom node extension that integrates UniRig and Make it Animatable for automated 3D skeleton extraction, rigging, and skinning of character meshes.
risk_notes: Installs via ComfyUI Manager or manual git clone/pip. Uses an experimental 'comfy-env' wrapper and the 'pixi' package manager to handle dependencies, which may modify local environment configurations. Requires significant local compute resources and access to the filesystem for model weights and 3D asset processing.

title: dannymcc/may
summary: A comprehensive, self-hosted vehicle management dashboard for tracking fuel, maintenance, expenses, and reminders with multi-user support and Home Assistant integration.
risk_notes: Installs via Docker or Python/pip. Requires persistent volume access for data storage. The application handles sensitive user data and credentials for notifications (email, webhooks). It is actively maintained and follows standard containerized deployment practices.

title: dev-lu/osint_toolkit
summary: A self-hosted, Docker-based OSINT platform that aggregates multiple threat intelligence APIs, email analysis tools, and AI-driven detection rule generation for security analysts.
risk_notes: Deployed via Docker Compose. Requires numerous third-party API keys (VirusTotal, Shodan, etc.) to function, which creates a significant surface area for credential management. The project is explicitly labeled as 'not production ready' and an 'early prototype', suggesting potential bugs or security vulnerabilities. It requires network access to reach external intelligence services.
